subject: Social Science (CBSE - Social and Political Life– II)

activities_projects.title: Research Project: Global Movements for Equality
activities_projects.objective: Compare equality struggles in India with other countries
steps: Select one Indian equality movement from the chapter
Research a similar movement in another country (e.g., Civil Rights Movement in the USA)
Prepare a comparative report highlighting causes, leaders, methods, and outcomes
Present your findings in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand global perspectives on equality and draw parallels
activities_projects.evaluation: Research depth, comparison accuracy, presentation clarity (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Data Analysis: Inequality Statistics
steps: Collect data on literacy rates, gender ratio, or employment across communities
Create charts or graphs to visualize inequalities
Write an analysis highlighting trends and possible reasons
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop quantitative analysis skills and understand societal disparities
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of data, analysis quality, and visualization (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mock UN Assembly on Equality
activities_projects.objective: Simulate international decision-making on social justice
steps: Assign each student a country or community to represent
Research their stance on equality and justice
Conduct a debate simulating UN discussions, drafting resolutions
Vote on proposals and summarize outcomes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand global governance and negotiation for equality
activities_projects.evaluation: Research quality, participation, negotiation skills (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Documentary Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Critically evaluate media portrayal of equality struggles
steps: Watch a documentary on social or gender equality movements
Identify biases, strengths, and weaknesses in presentation
Write a 1–2 page critical review linking to chapter concepts
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop media literacy and critical thinking
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of critique and link to chapter (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Case Study: Successful Grassroots Movements
steps: Identify a local or community-based initiative promoting equality
Interview organizers or collect information online
Prepare a detailed case study including challenges, methods, and outcomes
Share findings with class via presentation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand practical implementation of social change
activities_projects.evaluation: Research depth, analysis, presentation clarity (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Opinion Editorial (Op-Ed) Writing
activities_projects.objective: Express informed opinions on equality issues
steps: Choose a contemporary equality issue (e.g., gender pay gap, caste discrimination, child labor)
Research facts, statistics, and different viewpoints
Write a 300–350 word op-ed advocating solutions
Publish in school magazine or display on classroom board
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop persuasive writing and analytical skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Argument strength, evidence, writing clarity (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Digital Awareness Campaign
steps: Create a digital poster, short video, or infographic promoting equality
Include statistics, slogans, or quotes from reformers
Share it in class or school website/social media
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn digital communication and social responsibility
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, content accuracy, engagement potential (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Simulation: Law Making for Equality
activities_projects.objective: Understand legislative process to ensure equality
materials: Reference material on Indian Constitution and laws
steps: Choose a hypothetical issue of inequality in society
Draft a law or policy addressing it
Present your draft in a class 'Parliament' debate
Discuss potential impacts and challenges
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand law-making and policy planning for social justice
activities_projects.evaluation: Feasibility, legal understanding, presentation clarity (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ethical Dilemma Discussion
steps: Present a real-life scenario involving discrimination or bias
Discuss possible actions, consequences, and ethical considerations
Write a reflection summarizing your reasoning
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students strengthen ethical reasoning and empathy
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of reasoning and reflection clarity (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Extended Research Paper
activities_projects.objective: Conduct in-depth study on historical equality struggle
steps: Select a major historical struggle for equality in India
Research its background, leaders, resistance faced, and outcomes
Prepare a 5–6 page research paper with citations
Present key findings in class discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop research, analytical, and presentation skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Research depth, accuracy, writing quality, and presentation (15 marks)
